Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1, Dmitri's stipend audit email
Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "stipend" / "duplicate receipt" / "Dmitri" or similar)
Discover: email dmitri_receipt_audit_findings - [dmitri.volkov@moveops.com](mailto:dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) > [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com); subject: duplicate receipt anomalies across 3 clients; identifies STIP-2026-0089 Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89, PAID 2026-03-08), STIP-2026-0094 Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89, Pending), STIP-2026-0097 Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45, Pending). Records #1 and #2 are byte-for-byte identical PDFs (same SHA-256 hash). Record #3 shares the same PDF template ID (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041) and /Creator metadata.
Key: Establishes scope of the stipend fraud investigation - $89 already disbursed, $134 pending, 3 employees across 3 client companies.
OE 2, Marcus's response and admin holds
Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "Marcus" / "stipend" / "admin hold" / "STIP" or similar)
Discover: email email_marcus_qb_status_reply - Marcus confirms: STIP-2026-0089 already paid ($89 disbursed 2026-03-08), STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097 pending with admin holds placed 2026-03-15. Instructed Mina (Vectral contact) and Emeka (Mosaic contact) to reach out to client HR for receipt verification.
Key: Confirms one receipt paid before hold, two on hold. Establishes that Emeka was tasked with Mosaic/Tamara verification.
OE 3, Emeka's verification request to Tamara Byrd
Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "Andre Washington" / "Tamara" / "verification" / "receipt" or similar)
Discover: email email_emeka_to_tamara_receipt_verify - [emeka.diallo@moveops.com](mailto:emeka.diallo@moveops.com) > [tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com](mailto:tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com); sent 2026-03-15; asks Tamara to confirm: (1) whether Mosaic has internal record of Andre purchasing the keyboard, (2) whether TechComfort Supply is a recognized vendor, (3) whether Andre can provide additional documentation (shipping confirmation, order email).
Key: Verification request sent but agent must determine if Tamara ever responded.
OE 4, Search for Tamara's response
Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "tamara.byrd" / "Andre Washington" / "TechComfort" or similar)
Discover: No response from [tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com](mailto:tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com) exists in the email data. The verification request sent on March 15 has unanswered for over six weeks as of April 26.
Key: Critical finding - Tamara never came back on the Andre Washington verification. This is still hanging, answering the prompt's central question.
OE 5, Slack discussions on stipend audit
Read/lookup
Tool: conversations_search_messages (search_query: "stipend" / "receipt" / "duplicate" / "admin hold" or similar)
Discover: Slack messages in #finance and #engineering discussing the flagged receipts, Marcus's decision to place holds, and Dmitri's audit context. May also surface discussion about the ExpenseBot pause and the broader pilot audit (47 expenses, multiple false rejections due to stale default limits, 3 false approvals due to config bugs).
Key: Additional context on whether the duplicate receipts are fraud or a system artifact from the receipt template generator.
OE 6, Airtable stipend records
Read/lookup
Tool: airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (non-default) - base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001, search for "keyboard" / "monitor stand" / "Andre" / "Kevin" / "Jordan" or similar
Discover: rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard (Vectral, $89, Approved, hash a3f7c8d1...f0a2b4, flagged "Receipt hash identical to Andre Washington"), rec_stipend_andre_keyboard (Mosaic, $89, Pending Approval, same hash, flagged "Byte-for-byte identical PDF"), rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand (BrightLoop, $45, Pending Approval, different hash 7b9e1d3f...7e9b, flagged "PDF /Creator metadata identical and /Template field references exact same template ID"). Agent should also scan for additional anomalies beyond these three.
Key: Airtable records contain detailed audit flags with hash values and metadata - these are the records to update with investigation findings.
OE 7, QuickBooks stipend disbursement records
Read/lookup
Tool: quickbooks_search_bills (non-default) - query for "STIP" / "stipend" or similar
Discover: STIP-2026-0089 ($89, PAID, balance $0, disbursed 2026-03-08), STIP-2026-0094 ($89, balance $89 outstanding, pending approval), STIP-2026-0097 ($45, balance $45 outstanding, pending approval). The admin holds referenced in email/Slack correspond to these unpaid balances.
Key: Confirms the financial state - $89 already out the door, $134 frozen. Cross-references with Airtable records.
OE 8, QuickBooks - Swift Relocations bills
Read/lookup
Tool: quickbooks_search_bills (non-default) - query for "Swift" or vendor_swift or similar
Discover: bill_swift_tran (Kevin Tran, Portland > Denver, $2,885: $2,800 moving + $85 bike handling), bill_swift_venkatesh (Priya Venkatesh, Houston > Seattle, $4,450: $3,800 moving + $650 electronics/lab surcharge), bill_swift_kovac (Ines Kovac, Brooklyn > Austin, $3,675: $3,200 moving + $350 walkup + $125 electronics). Total: $11,010.
Key: These are the actual bills to compare against relocation records on file.
OE 9, Airtable - Swift relocation records
Read/lookup
Tool: airtable_search_records (non-default) - base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, search for "Swift" in Special Requirements or vendor fields
Discover: Multiple relocation records referencing Swift as the moving vendor, including Priya Venkatesh (Houston > Seattle, electronics surcharge $650 noted in special requirements), Kevin Tran (Portland > Denver), and others. Agent compares Airtable relocation details (coordinator assignments, special handling notes, move dates) against actual Swift QuickBooks bills to identify any discrepancies in scope or charges.
Key: Cross-referencing Airtable relocation records with QuickBooks bills allows agent to verify whether billed amounts align with the moves on file and flag any mismatches.
OE 10, Email - Swift pricing update
Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "Swift" / "pricing" / "rate increase" / "DOT surcharge" or similar)
Discover: Email from [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) > [chloe.vance@moveops.com](mailto:chloe.vance@moveops.com); subject "Pricing Update Effective Immediately - DOT Documentation Surcharge"; effective April 11, 2026: 15% base rate increase on all new bookings + $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment for compliance paperwork.
Key: Agent verifies the 15% and $500 figures match what the prompt states, and checks whether any bills post-April 11 reflect the new pricing.
OE 11, QuickBooks - Mosaic Robotics invoices
Read/lookup
Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices (non-default) - query for "Mosaic" or customer cust_mosaic or similar
Discover: INV-2026-0401 ($4,500, Andre Washington relocation), INV-2026-0306 ($10,000, Lena Kowalski + Andre Mitchell + equipment stipend), INV-2026-0411 ($9,010, 7 line items including prototype crating $1,200, hazmat handling $800, interim storage $450), CM-2026-0415 (-$90,000 credit memo for damaged robotic arm prototype, Zara Okoye). Total receivable: $23,510. Credit memo: -$90,000.
Key: The credit memo is critical for the net position - it represents the insurance claim for the damaged prototype.
OE 12, QuickBooks - Mosaic accruals and DOT penalty
Read/lookup
Tool: quickbooks_search_bills (non-default) - query for "ACCRUAL" / "Mosaic" / "DOT" or similar; also quickbooks_search_accounts for accrual account balances
Discover: ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000, contingent liability for damaged robotic arm Unit #1, Zara Okoye relocation - insurance covers $50,000, MoveOps direct exposure $40,000, status: DO NOT PAY, pending insurance adjudication). ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000, contingent DOT/PHMSA penalty for Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, triggered by missing placarding certificate on Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic laser cutter shipment). Account balances: acct_contingent_liability_claims = $90,000,acct_regulatory_compliance_expense = $50,000.
Key: Both accruals remain at originally booked amounts - no changes since booking. Agent confirms accuracy as requested.
OE 13, Emails/Slack - Mosaic damage claim and expense tool context
Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails / conversations_search_messages (query: "Mosaic" / "prototype" / "damage" / "expense" / "auto-categorizer" or similar)
Discover: Emeka's incident report on $90K robotic arm damage (cracked servo motor housing, joint calibration misalignment), insurance claim details ($50K coverage, $40K gap), Mosaic expense auto-categorizer false approvals - three records: (1) Andre Washington standing desk $1,200 approved against $800 policy limit, $400 overage (recStip000000021), (2) Andre Washington coworking membership $350, excluded category auto-approved in error (recStip000000022), (3) Kenji Nakamura coworking day passes $135, same excluded-category bug (recStip000000023). Total MoveOps cost absorption: ~$885. Slack discussions about the "expense tool mess" provide additional context on Mosaic's financial complexity.
Key: Feeds into the net position calculation - agent must factor in regular invoicing, credit memo, accruals, expense tool exposure, and stipend fraud.
OE 14, CRM - Mosaic company and deal data
Read/lookup
Tool: crm_search_companies (non-default) - name "Mosaic" or similar
Discover: company_mosaic (Mosaic Robotics, client, Tamara Byrd contact, Emeka Diallo AM). Multiple deals: deal_mosaic_q2 (Andre Washington, $4,500), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 (Zara Okoye, $2,700, risk flagged), deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 (Deshawn Hartley, $1,520), deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 (Keiko Tanaka, $2,770).
Key: Additional deal context for understanding the full Mosaic account picture.
OE 15, Linear - existing tracking tickets
Read/lookup
Tool: linear_list_issues (non-default) - query "stipend" / "Swift" / "Mosaic" / "vendor" / "receipt" or similar
Discover: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (receipt hash fingerprinting system, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: state_todo, team: team_engineering, priority P1). Also finds vendor strategy memo ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36, assigned to Marcus Thorne, team_operations) and vendor margin dataset ticket (linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170, actually assigned to Alejandro Fuentes). No dedicated ticket for: (1) resolving the actual flagged stipend receipts (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection is about building the detection system, not resolving the 3 cases), (2) Swift billing reconciliation, (3) Mosaic damage claim financial tracking, (4) following up on unanswered Tamara verification.
Key: Agent identifies which issues lack tracking tickets - these are the ones to create.
OE 16, Update Airtable stipend records with findings
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: airtable_update_records - base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001, records rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand
Action: Update each flagged stipend record with investigation findings - specifically: Tamara Byrd has not responded to verification request (sent March 15, unanswered as of April 26), fraud vs system artifact assessment based on evidence gathered, and current hold/disbursement status.
OE 17, Update Airtable relocation records with billing discrepancy notes
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: airtable_update_records - base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, relevant Swift-linked relocation records
Action: Add notes on billing discrepancies found during Swift reconciliation (e.g., Airtable vendor cost vs actual QuickBooks bill amounts).
OE 18, Post summary to #finance channel
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: conversations_add_message : channel_id C005 (#finance)
Action: Post consolidated summary of all open items covering: (1) stipend audit status 3 flagged receipts, 1 paid, 2 on hold, Tamara verification unanswered, (2) Swift reconciliation - bill totals vs relocation records, pricing update impact, (3) Mosaic net position : receivables, credit memo, accruals, expense exposure. Written so Alejandro can pick up anything Hana misses.
OE 19, Email Marcus the full picture
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: send_email : sender [hana.kim@moveops.com](mailto:hana.kim@moveops.com), to [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com); subject covering stipend / Swift / Mosaic reconciliation (or similar)
Action: Email Marcus the complete picture covering the stipend situation (flagged receipts, Tamara non-response, fraud assessment), Swift reconciliation (bills vs records, pricing impact), and Mosaic position (net receivable/payable, accrual accuracy, expense tool exposure). Marcus needs this before his meeting with Elena.
OE 20, Create Linear tickets for untracked issues
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: linear_create_issue : team appropriate (team_operations or team_finance or similar)
Action: Create tracking ticket(s) for vendor/expense issues that lack dedicated tickets : e.g., Swift billing reconciliation follow-up, Mosaic damage claim financial resolution, and/or Tamara Byrd receipt verification follow-up (over 5 weeks unanswered). Ensures nothing falls through the cracks as requested.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1, Dmitri's stipend audit email

OE 11, QuickBooks - Mosaic Robotics invoices
Read/lookup
Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices (non-default) - query for "Mosaic" or customer cust_mosaic or similar
Discover: INV-2026-0401 ($4,500, Andre Washington relocation), INV-2026-0306 ($10,000, Lena Kowalski + Andre Mitchell + equipment stipend), INV-2026-0411 ($9,010, 7 line items including prototype crating $1,200, hazmat handling $800, interim storage $450), CM-2026-0415 (-$90,000 credit memo for damaged robotic arm prototype, Zara Okoye). Total receivable: $23,510. Credit memo: -$90,000.
Key: The credit memo is critical for the net position - it represents the insurance claim for the damaged prototype.
OE 12, QuickBooks - Mosaic accruals and DOT penalty
Read/lookup
Tool: quickbooks_search_bills (non-default) - query for "ACCRUAL" / "Mosaic" / "DOT" or similar; also quickbooks_search_accounts for accrual account balances
Discover: ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000, contingent liability for damaged robotic arm Unit #1, Zara Okoye relocation - insurance covers $50,000, MoveOps direct exposure $40,000, status: DO NOT PAY, pending insurance adjudication). ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000, contingent DOT/PHMSA penalty for Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, triggered by missing placarding certificate on Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic laser cutter shipment). Account balances: acct_contingent_liability_claims = $90,000,acct_regulatory_compliance_expense = $50,000.
Key: Both accruals remain at originally booked amounts - no changes since booking. Agent confirms accuracy as requested.
OE 13, Emails/Slack - Mosaic damage claim and expense tool context
Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails / conversations_search_messages (query: "Mosaic" / "prototype" / "damage" / "expense" / "auto-categorizer" or similar)
Discover: Emeka's incident report on $90K robotic arm damage (cracked servo motor housing, joint calibration misalignment), insurance claim details ($50K coverage, $40K gap), Mosaic expense auto-categorizer false approvals - three records: (1) Andre Washington standing desk $1,200 approved against $800 policy limit, $400 overage (recStip000000021), (2) Andre Washington coworking membership $350, excluded category auto-approved in error (recStip000000022), (3) Kenji Nakamura coworking day passes $135, same excluded-category bug (recStip000000023). Total MoveOps cost absorption: ~$885. Slack discussions about the "expense tool mess" provide additional context on Mosaic's financial complexity.
Key: Feeds into the net position calculation - agent must factor in regular invoicing, credit memo, accruals, expense tool exposure, and stipend fraud.
OE 14, CRM - Mosaic company and deal data
Read/lookup
Tool: crm_search_companies (non-default) - name "Mosaic" or similar
Discover: company_mosaic (Mosaic Robotics, client, Tamara Byrd contact, Emeka Diallo AM). Multiple deals: deal_mosaic_q2 (Andre Washington, $4,500), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 (Zara Okoye, $2,700, risk flagged), deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 (Deshawn Hartley, $1,520), deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 (Keiko Tanaka, $2,770).
Key: Additional deal context for understanding the full Mosaic account picture.
OE 15, Linear - existing tracking tickets
Read/lookup
Tool: linear_list_issues (non-default) - query "stipend" / "Swift" / "Mosaic" / "vendor" / "receipt" or similar
Discover: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (receipt hash fingerprinting system, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: state_todo, team: team_engineering, priority P1). Also finds vendor strategy memo ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36, assigned to Marcus Thorne, team_operations) and vendor margin dataset ticket (linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170, actually assigned to Alejandro Fuentes). No dedicated ticket for: (1) resolving the actual flagged stipend receipts (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection is about building the detection system, not resolving the 3 cases), (2) Swift billing reconciliation, (3) Mosaic damage claim financial tracking, (4) following up on unanswered Tamara verification.
Key: Agent identifies which issues lack tracking tickets - these are the ones to create.
OE 16, Update Airtable stipend records with findings
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: airtable_update_records - base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001, records rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand
Action: Update each flagged stipend record with investigation findings - specifically: Tamara Byrd has not responded to verification request (sent March 15, unanswered as of April 26), fraud vs system artifact assessment based on evidence gathered, and current hold/disbursement status.
OE 17, Update Airtable relocation records with billing discrepancy notes
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: airtable_update_records - base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, relevant Swift-linked relocation records
Action: Add notes on billing discrepancies found during Swift reconciliation (e.g., Airtable vendor cost vs actual QuickBooks bill amounts).
OE 18, Post summary to #finance channel
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: conversations_add_message : channel_id C005 (#finance)
Action: Post consolidated summary of all open items covering: (1) stipend audit status 3 flagged receipts, 1 paid, 2 on hold, Tamara verification unanswered, (2) Swift reconciliation - bill totals vs relocation records, pricing update impact, (3) Mosaic net position : receivables, credit memo, accruals, expense exposure. Written so Alejandro can pick up anything Hana misses.
OE 19, Email Marcus the full picture
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: send_email : sender [hana.kim@moveops.com](mailto:hana.kim@moveops.com), to [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com); subject covering stipend / Swift / Mosaic reconciliation (or similar)
Action: Email Marcus the complete picture covering the stipend situation (flagged receipts, Tamara non-response, fraud assessment), Swift reconciliation (bills vs records, pricing impact), and Mosaic position (net receivable/payable, accrual accuracy, expense tool exposure). Marcus needs this before his meeting with Elena.
OE 20, Create Linear tickets for untracked issues
WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: linear_create_issue : team appropriate (team_operations or team_finance or similar)
Action: Create tracking ticket(s) for vendor/expense issues that lack dedicated tickets : e.g., Swift billing reconciliation follow-up, Mosaic damage claim financial resolution, and/or Tamara Byrd receipt verification follow-up (over 5 weeks unanswered). Ensures nothing falls through the cracks as requested.

tool selection

✓Run #5The agent called airtable_list_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Relocations', satisfying this criterion.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_list_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Relocations', satisfying the requirement to retrieve relocation records from this base.
2 The model must use the quickbooks_search_bills tool to look up vendor bills (e.g., Swift Relocations bills and/or stipend disbursement records).
tool selection
 —✓✓—✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering by VendorRef LIKE '%Swift%' to retrieve Swift Relocations bills.

—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_bills multiple times: once with VendorRef = 'vendor_swift', once with VendorRef LIKE '%Swift%', and once with no criteria (fetchAll:true). These calls retrieved the Swift Relocations bills.

tool selection

11 The final response addresses whether the flagged receipts represent actual fraud or a system artifact from the receipt template, referencing at least one of: matching SHA-256 hash, identical PDF metadata, template ID TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, or ExpenseBot configuration issues.
outcome
 —✓✓—✓✓ 100%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The agent's assessment in the response, Airtable updates, Slack message, and email all reference the system artifact explanation, citing TechComfort Supply's standardized invoice generator (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041 template), SHA-256 hash, and PDF metadata as technical evidence.
✓Run #3The final response states 'Most likely a system artifact from TechComfort Supply's invoice generator (same template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041), NOT deliberate fraud.' The Airtable updates reference the SHA-256 hash, PDF metadata, and template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041 as technical evidence.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The final response and Airtable updates reference the SHA-256 hash (byte-for-byte identical files), PDF metadata, and template ID TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, concluding it is 'most likely a system artifact from TechComfort's invoice generator, not fraud.'
✓Run #6The agent references 'byte-for-byte identical PDF', 'same SHA-256 hash', 'TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1', and template ID 'TC-RECEIPT-STD-041' in the Airtable updates and email. The final response also references the shared template and identical hash, assessing it as likely a system artifact vs. coordinated fraud.

29 The final response synthesizes the Mosaic Robotics net position, indicating that MoveOps is in a net payable position to Mosaic (receivables of approximately $23,510 minus the $90,000 credit memo results in a net payable of approximately $66,490, or similar net calculation showing the credit memo far exceeds receivables).
outcome

✗Run #2The agent does not explicitly synthesize a net payable position showing Mosaic owes MoveOps (or MoveOps owes Mosaic) a specific net figure like ~$66,490 (receivables minus credit memo). Instead, the agent lists receivables (~$24,010) and contingent liabilities ($90K + $50K + $1,550) separately without netting out receivables against the $90K credit memo to produce a single net position figure.
✓Run #3The final response and Slack message synthesize the Mosaic net position showing receivables of $23,510 minus the $90,000 credit memo and $50,000 DOT accrual resulting in 'Worst-case net: -$118,440', clearly showing MoveOps is in a net payable position to Mosaic. The criterion accepts any reasonable net figure accounting for receivables and credit memo.
—Run #4No justification
✗Run #5The agent's final response and email present best/moderate/worst case scenarios but do not clearly present a net payable calculation of ~$66,490 (receivables ~$23,510 minus $90,000 credit memo). The agent reports $24,010 in receivables (not $23,510) and bundles the credit memo into 'damage accrual' rather than computing the discrete net. The worst-case calculation of ~$67,675 adds the $50K DOT penalty, not just the credit memo vs receivables.
✓Run #6The final response and email both show a net position calculation. The email presents receivables of $23,510 against exposure of $140,885 (including CM-2026-0415 at -$90,000 credit memo), resulting in 'MoveOps is approximately $117,375 in the red on Mosaic.' The credit memo is accounted for in the calculation, showing MoveOps is in a net payable/negative position.
